exemplar.title: Modelling the Cooling rate of Chamomile Tea

detailedExemplarData.title: Modelling the Cooling rate of Chamomile Tea

subject.title: Mathematics Applications & Interpretation (AI)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics: Applications and Interpretation** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **real-world applications of mathematics, statistical analysis, mathematical modeling, and data interpretation**, with a strong focus on **technology use, practical problem-solving, and interpreting mathematical results in context**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | External examination (calculator permitted) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 2** | External examination (technology required) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | External examination (technology required) | N/A | 1h | HL: 20% |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal assessment | 30h | 30h | 20% |

strengths: Clear logical development following a coherent IA structure (introduction, modelling, evaluation).
Well-organized with headings, numbered tables/figures, and a comprehensive contents page.
Mostly concise and relevant material with directly supporting discussion and minimal unnecessary content.
weaknesses: Minor lapses in narrative flow (occasional digressions and grammar issues) interrupt seamless progression.
Repeated explanations of the test rationale add unnecessary length.
Formatting issues such as duplicated page numbers in the contents and suboptimal placement of table captions reduce polish.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Band-3 descriptors best match this work: the exploration is coherent with clear logical development, well organized into sections, and mostly concise. Occasional digressions, minor grammar problems, and redundant explanations prevent a band-4 award. Hence, a score of 3/4 accurately reflects the presentation quality.

strengths: Mathematical notation (e.g. T(t), e^{-kt}, ln, MAE) is used correctly in most cases with accompanying explanations.
Effective use of multiple representations: data tables, scatter plots, function plots, and linearised graphs.
Communication is generally clear with consistent use of symbols and narrative interpretation of results.
weaknesses: Inconsistent formatting (misaligned tables, stray symbols) and occasional casual wording (âconstant proportionalityâ).
Missing axis labels and units on graphs reduce immediate interpretability.
Redundant equations and lack of a clear, unified rounding policy interrupt the flow.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work meets band-3 criteria: mathematical language and notation are mostly appropriate, representations are used effectively, and communication is clear with minor inconsistencies. Gaps in precision, formatting glitches, and inconsistent wording prevent a band-4 evaluation. Therefore, 3/4 is justified.

strengths: The investigation is driven by a personal motivation (optimising study routines), showing clear independent thinking.
Designing a bespoke experiment comparing three modelling techniques demonstrates initiative beyond a textbook approach.
Linking results back to daily life and study habits reflects meaningful personal engagement.
weaknesses: While novel, the approach does not show the level of originality or depth required for outstanding engagement.
Some choices follow standard IA prompts rather than wholly unique directions.
Further personal reflections on decision-making or methodological choices could deepen engagement.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Band-2 descriptors align with the evidence of significant independent thinking, a clear personal approach, and demonstrable creativity. However, the work stops short of the outstanding originality needed for band-3. Thus, a score of 2/3 accurately represents personal engagement.

strengths: Meaningful reflection on experimental limitations and modelling assumptions is present throughout.
Basic critique of model accuracy (error measures, model divergence) demonstrates reflective analysis.
Connections between mathematical findings and practical recommendations (brew time, study optimisation) are clear.
weaknesses: Reflection remains surface-level; deeper critical analysis and exploration of implications are limited.
Additional justification or derivation explanations (e.g., optimum temperature) would strengthen the reflective depth.
Opportunities to challenge underlying assumptions or alternative approaches are not fully explored.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration shows meaningful reflection and critique consistent with band-2 descriptors. While connections to understanding are clear, the analysis lacks the depth and critical rigour required for band-3. A score of 2/3 therefore reflects the reflection quality.

strengths: Appropriate application of exponential decay modelling, linearisation, and Newtonâs law of cooling at HL AI course level.
Mathematical calculations (parameter estimation and MAE) are correct with only minor rounding inconsistencies.
Demonstrates good conceptual understanding of model development, parameter interpretation, and error analysis.
weaknesses: Minor rounding inconsistencies and lack of a consistent rounding policy.
Mathematical derivations are sometimes reproduced rather than developed with deeper rigour.
Statistical measures of variability (e.g. standard deviation) are omitted, limiting the contextual depth of data analysis.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Use of relevant course-level mathematics effectively aligns with band-4 descriptors: calculations are mostly correct, and understanding is good. The work is not in the highest band due to minor errors, limited statistical depth, and lack of extended rigour. Thus, a score of 4/6 appropriately represents the mathematical use.

strandSelections.reasoning: Designing a personal experiment, deciding to compare three modelling techniques and introducing MAE exhibit significant independent thinking, though not outstanding originality or depth.  Band 2 therefore captures the quality shown.

Explanations of model choice, parameter estimation and error analysis reveal good conceptual grasp across the exploration.  Some derivations are reproduced rather than developed rigorously, so the level stays at âgood understanding throughoutâ rather than the highest bands.

Calculations of k, model equations and MAE have been carried out correctly; minor rounding inconsistencies are present but do not materially affect conclusions.  This matches âmathematics correct with minor errorsâ (band 4).

Most material directly supports the investigation; a few extended quotations and repeated explanations of formulae add needless length.  Overall it is relevant and mostly concise, satisfying the band-3 descriptor.

Mathematical symbols (T(t), e^{-kt}, ln, MAE) are used correctly in nearly all cases and narrative explanations accompany them.  Some casual wording ("k = constant proportionality") and inconsistent italicisation stop it reaching the âconsistent and accurate throughoutâ standard, so band 3 is appropriate.

Explanations, diagrams and notation are generally clear.  A few formatting glitches (mis-aligned appendix table, stray symbols) create minor inconsistencies, so the work is âclear and mostly consistentâ (band 3).

Exponential decay, data linearisation and Newtonâs law of cooling are all within HL AI syllabus and are applied purposefully.  The mathematics supports the investigation effectively but is not especially sophisticated or extended into calculus beyond first-order differential use, hence band 4 (ârelevant maths at course level used effectivelyâ).

The student employs tables of raw/mean data, scatter plots, function plots and a linearised ln(Tâ30) graph.  These multiple representations are used effectively to support arguments, yet remain routine rather than sophisticated; hence band 3 fits best.

The evaluation section discusses experimental limitations, modelling assumptions and possible improvements, giving meaningfulâbut not deeply criticalâreflection throughout. This aligns with the band-2 descriptor.

Using linearisation, MAE, and linking results to a daily routine shows creativity and initiative beyond a purely textbook application, but not at a level that could be called âoutstandingâ. Band 2 is justified.

The report follows the classic IA sequence (introduction â plan â modelling â comparison â conclusion â evaluation) and ideas progress naturally from data collection to model construction and checking.  Minor lapses in narrative flow (occasional digressions/grammar problems) prevent it from feeling âseamlessâ, but the logical thread is clear and sustained, matching the descriptor for a coherent piece with clear logical development rather than âexcellentâ.

Error measures are calculated and models compared; the student comments on why certain models diverge.  The analysis shows some critique but remains fairly surface-level, so band 2 (âmeaningful analysis with some critiqueâ) is most fitting.

Connections between mathematical findings and the practical goal (when to brew tea, optimal study time) are clearly made, enhancing understanding, yet the discussion is not thorough enough for the top band.  Band 2 is appropriate.

Headings, numbered tables/figures and a contents page demonstrate good structure; introduction, body sections for each model, and a concluding evaluation are all present.  Transitions are sensible, yet not especially sophisticated, hence "well-organised" rather than "exceptionally well organised".

The studentâs motivation (optimising study and sleep) shapes data collection and final recommendations, demonstrating a clear personal approach that drives the exploration, consistent with band-2 descriptors.

annotations.comment: The raw data tables are well labeled with clear trial numbers and units.
The rationale for repeating the test appears twice; condense these explanations to improve conciseness.
Introduce symbolic notation (e.g. T(t), t) when describing variables to enhance mathematical communication.
Consider adding a concise research question immediately after the title to guide the reader and improve clarity.
The âPage Countâ note is administrative; it could be removed to maintain relevance and conciseness.
The contents page is comprehensive and provides a clear roadmap of the explorationâs structure.
Align table cell widths in the contents list for a more consistent and polished presentation.
Listing page â21â for both Conclusion and Evaluation may confuse the reader; consider differentiating or consolidating entries.
The introduction demonstrates strong personal engagement by linking study habits with the experimentâs motivation.
The descriptive discussion of apigenin is interesting but could be strengthened with quantitative context or data.
When citing the optimum temperature of 57.8 Â°C, briefly explain its derivation or reference to deepen the readerâs understanding.
The plan of action clearly outlines the methodology and trial structure, aiding coherence.
The title clearly identifies the focus on modeling the cooling rate of chamomile tea, providing immediate context.
Table 1âs caption is placed below; conventionally, move table titles above each table to improve readability.
Consider reporting variability measures (e.g. standard deviation) alongside means to contextualize data spread.
The formula $$\bar{x}=\frac{\sum x}{n}$$ is correctly presented and applied.
Redundant equations (e.g. â\bar{x}=meanâ) interrupt the flow; consolidate notation explanations in one place.
Present the mean computations for t=5 in a concise worked example or table for clarity.
Graph 1 effectively displays mean temperature data as a basis for modeling.
The graph lacks axis labels and units, reducing immediate interpretability.
Include axis titles with units and a legend on each graph to ensure clarity and self-containment.
The new model $$T(t)=Ce^{-kt}+T_s$$ correctly integrates the ambient temperature as an asymptote.
The term âproportinalityâ is misspelled and âconstant proportionalityâ is vague; use precise mathematical language.
Define each variable (C, k, T_s) immediately in text alongside the equation to reinforce notation clarity.
The limitations section thoughtfully acknowledges real-life variability not captured by the model.
The spurious notation â\overline{1}â appears mid-calculation and disrupts the logical sequence.
Reformat the logarithmic derivation steps in a clear, linear sequence to prevent misinterpretation of working.
The final model $$T(t)=39.3e^{-0.0307t}+30$$ is clearly stated and integrated with data.
The coefficient is presented with excessive precision, conflicting with the stated rounding policy.
Clearly state and apply a consistent rounding policy in both derivation and graphical plots.
